# Stock Analysis Financial Review

> Fundamental + technical investment-analysis workflow for publicly traded stocks. Each ticker gets
> a dated folder holding a 10-section investment memo (.docx), a 4-sheet valuation model (.xlsx
> where applicable), and the build script that generated them — driven end to end by the packaged
> stock-analysis skill.

## Start here
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): canonical agent guide — file map, per-analysis folder contract, verification gates.
- [README.md](README.md): human overview — methodology, analyses index table, build-script prerequisites.
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): commit + README standard.
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): project instructions / workflow context for agents.

## Engine
- [stock-analysis.skill](stock-analysis.skill): packaged Cowork skill driving an analysis end to end — research, model build, memo build.
- [_Analysis_Patterns/README.md](_Analysis_Patterns/README.md): reusable analysis patterns / methodology reference.

## Analyses
- [BROS/BROS-2026-06-05/create_bros_memo.js](BROS/BROS-2026-06-05/create_bros_memo.js): Dutch Bros memo generator (memo .docx beside it).
- [SG/SG-2026-06-05/create_sg_memo.js](SG/SG-2026-06-05/create_sg_memo.js): Sweetgreen memo generator; model built by [SG/SG-2026-06-05/build_sg_model.py](SG/SG-2026-06-05/build_sg_model.py).
- [NOW/NOW-2026-06-05/create_now_memo.js](NOW/NOW-2026-06-05/create_now_memo.js): ServiceNow memo generator (memo + model beside it).
- [PLTR/PLTR-2026-06-05/build_model.py](PLTR/PLTR-2026-06-05/build_model.py): Palantir model generator (memo + model beside it).

## Key rules
- Never overwrite a dated analysis — a re-run gets a new {TICKER}-{YYYY-MM-DD}/ folder; superseded ones move to {TICKER}_Archive/.
- Memo is always 10 sections; model is the 4-sheet layout (financials, projections, DCF, options).
- DCF is 3-scenario (bull/base/bear) with a single WACC; EBITDA-terminal for pre-profitable names.
- Keep the README analyses table in sync (ticker, company, date, rating, DCF bull/base/bear).
- Public repo: never commit brokerage credentials, account numbers, position sizes, PII, or API keys; provider keys come from env vars only.
- Commits follow CONTRIBUTING.md; README updated on every feat/data commit.
